name: Carmalo Truiand
bio:
Physical Appearance: Imperial male. Wears Imperial Watch boots, cuirass, gauntlets, and greaves. Armed with a silver warhammer.
Race: Imperial
Personality Keywords: smart, inexperienced, dutiful, promising
Role: An Imperial Watch Captain who replaces Audens Avidius as commander of the Northeast Watch Tower following the exposure of Avidius's corruption. He is described as "smart but inexperienced" and considered a good second choice for the position of Captain of the Guard in Anvil (after Itius Hayn). He inherits the patrol duties and responsibilities of his disgraced predecessor.
Home Location Information: The Captain's Quarters in the Northeast Watch Tower in the Imperial City.
Daily Routine: Carmalo sleeps in his quarters from 10pm to 6am, then eats breakfast in his room. At 8am he begins his morning patrol of the Market District, starting at the door to the Arena District and moving counterclockwise through the area—to the city isle exit at 9am, the Elven Gardens District gate at 10am, and the Green Emperor Way entrance at 11am. At noon he goes to the Feed Bag for a two-hour lunch. His afternoon patrol of the Arena District begins at the Market District gate, moving behind the Arena at 3pm and to the Arboretum gate at 4pm. At 5pm he returns to the Northeast Watch Tower, wandering the main floor until 6pm when he heads upstairs for dinner. At 8pm he returns downstairs to wander until bedtime at 10pm.
Personality - The New Captain: Carmalo Truiand represents a fresh start for the Northeast Watch Tower after the corruption of Audens Avidius. While acknowledged as intelligent, his inexperience is noted by those who evaluate him—he has potential but hasn't yet proven himself in the way that veterans like Itius Hayn have. Taking over in the shadow of a corrupt predecessor must weigh heavily on him, and he dutifully follows the same patrol routes and schedule, perhaps trying to restore order and legitimacy to a position that was badly tarnished. He is a man with much to prove, stepping into a role under difficult circumstances with his competence yet to be fully demonstrated.
author: Bethesda
